title: brachyscome basaltica var. gracilis
intro: Brachyscome basaltica var. gracilis, also known as the Tasmanian daisy, is a flowering plant native to the island of Tasmania in Australia. It belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Brachyscome basaltica var. gracilis can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and flowers can be used to make herbal remedies, although its effectiveness and safety may vary depending on the individual and the specific preparation method.
